title: A Popular History of Ireland
description: A Popular History of Ireland — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "A Popular History of Ireland ： from the Earliest Period to the Emancipation of t" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Complete table of contents for A Popular History of Ireland (1863) by Thomas D\''Arcy McGee'

memes.label: Act of Union 1800
memes.description: 'The legislative union of Great Britain and Ireland, passed in 1800 and effective January 1, 1801, abolishing the Irish Parliament.'
aliases: Union of Great Britain and Ireland

memes.label: 'George Browne, Archbishop of Dublin'
memes.description: 'First Protestant Archbishop of Dublin, appointed by Henry VIII to advance the English Reformation in Ireland.'

memes.label: 'James Ussher'
memes.description: 'Archbishop of Armagh and Primate of All Ireland, renowned scholar and theologian, author of the Ussher chronology.'

memes.label: Armagh
memes.description: 'The ecclesiastical capital of Ireland, seat of the Primate of All Ireland, founded by Saint Patrick.'

memes.label: Art McMurrogh
memes.description: 'Leinster king who led a sustained campaign of resistance against Richard II and English rule in Ireland during the late 14th and early 15th centuries.'

memes.label: Art O'Neil
memes.description: 'Irish chieftain of the O\'Neil dynasty who died during Hugh Roe O\'Donnell\'s dramatic escape from Dublin Castle in 1592.'

memes.label: Arthur O'Conor
memes.description: 'United Irishmen leader, radical MP for Phillipstown, nephew of Lord Longueville; arrested in 1798 and later served as a general under Napoleon.'

memes.label: Arthur O'Leary
memes.description: 'Irish Capuchin friar, polemicist, and writer of the late 18th century who opposed the Whiteboys and later supported the Volunteer movement.'

memes.label: Barry Yelverton
memes.description: 'Irish politician and judge who played a prominent role in Grattan''s Parliament, notably moving the repeal of Poynings'' Law and supporting legislative independence for Ireland.'

memes.label: Battle of Clontarf
memes.description: 'The decisive battle fought on Good Friday, 1014, in which Brian Boru defeated the Norse-Irish alliance.'

memes.label: Book of Rights
memes.description: 'An ancient Irish legal text compiled under the auspices of Saint Patrick, serving as the constitutional foundation of early Gaelic Ireland.'

memes.label: Brehon Law
memes.description: 'The native legal system of Ireland, administered by Brehons (judges), in use from pre-Christian times until the 17th century.'

memes.label: Brian Boru
memes.description: 'High King of Ireland (1002–1014) who broke Norse power at the Battle of Clontarf.'

memes.label: Brian O'Neil
memes.description: 'King of Tir Eoghan, acknowledged as Ard-Righ (High King) of Ireland at the conference of Cael-uisge in 1258, killed at the Battle of Down in 1260.'

memes.label: Cahir O'Doherty
memes.description: 'Irish chieftain of Innishowen whose 1608 rebellion and death provided the pretext for the Plantation of Ulster.'

memes.label: Cathal O'Conor
memes.description: 'King of Connaught in the early 13th century, known as Cathal Crovdearg (of the Red Hand), who allied with King John and died at the Battle of Down.'

memes.label: Catholic Association
memes.description: 'The mass political organization founded by Daniel OConnell in 1823 to campaign for Catholic Emancipation.'

memes.label: Catholic Emancipation
memes.description: 'The removal of legal disabilities against Catholics in the United Kingdom, achieved in 1829 through the leadership of Daniel OConnell.'

memes.label: Charles Blount, Lord Mountjoy
memes.description: 'English soldier-statesman and Lord Deputy of Ireland who defeated Hugh ONeill and ended the Nine Years War during the closing years of Elizabeth I.'

memes.label: Charles I
memes.description: 'King of England, Scotland, and Ireland (1625–1649), whose reign saw the Irish Rebellion of 1641, the Confederate Wars, and his own execution, precipitating the Cromwellian conquest of Ireland.'

memes.label: Charles II
memes.description: 'Charles II (1630–1685) was King of England, Scotland, and Ireland from the Restoration of 1660 until his death, whose reign saw the consolidation of the Protestant Ascendancy in Ireland.'

memes.label: 'Earl of Clanrickarde'
memes.description: 'Anglo-Irish noble title held by the Burke family in Connaught; key players in Tudor and Stuart Irish politics from the 16th through the 17th century.'

memes.label: Con O'Neil
memes.description: 'Lord of Tyrone (1483–1491), married Lady Alice Fitzgerald, one of the first Irish chiefs to accept an English title under Henry VIII.'

memes.label: Conor O'Brien
memes.description: 'King of Munster in the early 12th century, surnamed Conor "of the fortresses," a Dalcassian prince who contended with Thorlogh O''Conor for the high kingship of Ireland.'

memes.label: Cormac McCarthy
memes.description: 'Prince of Desmond who captured Waterford from the Normans in 1170 and led resistance in Munster during the Anglo-Norman invasion.'

memes.label: Cromwellian Settlement
memes.description: 'The Cromwellian Settlement (1652–1660) was the comprehensive confiscation and redistribution of Irish land following Oliver Cromwell''s conquest, permanently altering the pattern of landownership in Ireland.'

memes.label: Daniel O'Connell
memes.description: 'Irish political leader known as "The Liberator" who secured Catholic Emancipation in 1829.'

memes.label: Defenders
memes.description: 'The Defenders were a Catholic secret society and agrarian movement active in late 18th-century Ireland, originating in County Armagh as a defensive response to Protestant "Peep o'' Day Boys" raids, and later evolving into a revolutionary organization that allied with the United Irishmen in the Rebellion of 1798.'

memes.label: Dermid McMurrogh
memes.description: "Dermid McMurrogh (c.1110-1171) was King of Leinster whose appeal to Henry II of England for military assistance in 1166 led directly to the Anglo-Norman invasion of Ireland."

memes.label: Donald O'Neil
memes.description: 'Prince of Ulster, chief mover of the Bruce Invasion of Ireland (1315–1318), and author of the celebrated Remonstrance to Pope John XXII.'

memes.label: Donnell More O'Donnell
memes.description: 'Lord of Tyrconnell from 1208 to 1241 whose long career saw the consolidation of O`Donnell power in the northwest of Ireland.'

memes.label: Donogh O'Brien
memes.description: 'Son of Brian Boru, King of Munster after the Battle of Clontarf, rival claimant to the high kingship.'

memes.label: Dublin
memes.description: 'The capital and largest city of Ireland, founded as a Norse settlement and developed as the center of English administration.'

memes.label: Duke of Berwick
memes.description: 'Illegitimate son of James II who served as nominal Commander-in-Chief of the Jacobite army during the Williamite War in Ireland.'

memes.label: Earl of Desmond
memes.description: 'The Earls of Desmond were a branch of the Anglo-Norman Geraldine family who held vast territories in Munster from the 14th to the 16th centuries, playing a central role in the struggle between English Crown authority and Irish lordship.'

memes.label: Earl of Kildare
memes.description: 'The Earls of Kildare were the junior but more politically successful branch of the Geraldine family, dominating the English administration in Ireland from the 15th to the 16th centuries as Lords Deputies and effectively ruling Ireland on behalf of the Crown.'

memes.label: Early Irish Church
memes.description: 'The Early Irish Church was the Christian church established in Ireland by Saint Patrick and his successors, characterized by its monastic organization, missionary zeal, and distinctive traditions that flourished from the fifth through the twelfth centuries.'

memes.label: Edmund Burke
memes.description: 'Edmund Burke (1729–1797) was an Irish-born statesman, political philosopher, and orator who served as a Member of the British Parliament and is regarded as the father of modern conservatism.'

memes.label: Edward Bruce
memes.description: 'Brother of Robert the Bruce, crowned King of Ireland in 1316, led the Bruce Invasion of Ireland (1315–1318).'

memes.label: Edward Byrne
memes.description: 'Chairman of the Catholic Committee in the 1790s, prominent Dublin merchant, ally of John Keogh and Wolfe Tone.'

memes.label: 'Lord Edward Fitzgerald'
memes.description: 'Irish aristocrat and revolutionary who served as Commander-in-Chief of the United Irishmen; brother of the Duke of Leinster, a key leader of the 1798 Rebellion.'

memes.label: Edward VI
memes.description: 'Tudor king of England and Ireland whose short reign advanced the Protestant Reformation in both kingdoms and saw the first systematic imposition of the English liturgy on the Irish Church.'

memes.label: Elizabeth I
memes.description: 'Queen of England (1558–1603) whose reign saw the consolidation of English power in Ireland through plantation and conquest.'

memes.label: Father Kearns
memes.description: 'Catholic priest and rebel leader in the 1798 Rebellion who led the Wexford insurgents alongside Father Clinch, fought in Kildare, and was executed at Edenderry.'

memes.label: Feagh Mac Hugh O'Byrne
memes.description: 'Wicklow chieftain, known as "the firebrand of the mountains," key ally of Hugh ONeill in the Nine Years War.'

memes.label: Felim mac Crimthann
memes.description: 'King of Munster in the ninth century, prominent opponent of the Norse invasions and one of the dominant Irish rulers of his era'

memes.label: Felim O'Conor
memes.description: 'King of Connaught in the 13th century who resisted the de Burgh lordship and allied with Edward Bruce during the Scottish invasion of Ireland (1315–1318).'

memes.label: Flan of the Shannon
memes.description: 'Flan of the Shannon (Flan Siona), High King of Ireland from 877 to 916, whose 38-year reign spanned the late 9th and early 10th centuries and was marked by the reduction of Munster, family tragedy, and a generally peaceful rule.'

memes.label: Florence McCarthy
memes.description: 'Two notable MacCarthy chieftains of Desmond: the 13th-century victor of Callan-Glen and the Elizabethan-era Lord of Carberry who allied with Hugh ONeill.'

memes.label: Gilbert de Clare
memes.description: 'A 12th-century Norman lord, father of Richard de Clare (Strongbow), Governor of Pembroke for nearly forty years, and holder of the powerful lordship of Chepstow.'

memes.label: Grey de Wilton
memes.description: 'English nobleman and Lord Deputy of Ireland (1580–1582) who presided over the massacre at Smerwick during the Second Geraldine League.'

memes.label: 'Henry Flood'
memes.description: 'Irish statesman and orator, leader of the Patriot Party in the Irish House of Commons and rival of Henry Grattan, instrumental in securing legislative independence in 1782.'

memes.label: Henry Grattan
memes.description: 'Henry Grattan (1746–1820) was an Irish statesman and orator who led the movement for Irish legislative independence and opposed the Act of Union.'

relations.label: Union of Great Britain and Ireland

memes.label: Henry II
memes.description: 'King of England (1154–1189) whose intervention in Ireland initiated the Anglo-Norman presence.'

memes.label: Henry Ireton
memes.description: "Henry Ireton (1611–1651) was an English general and statesman who served as Cromwell's son-in-law and Lord Deputy of Ireland during the Cromwellian conquest."

memes.label: Henry Joy McCracken
memes.description: 'Belfast cotton manufacturer and United Irishman leader in Ulster who commanded the rebel forces at the Battle of Antrim in 1798, executed after the rebellion.'

memes.label: Henry Sidney
memes.description: 'English statesman who served three times as Lord Deputy of Ireland during the Tudor conquest, architect of the provincial presidency system and the policy of military subjugation of Gaelic Ireland.'

memes.label: Henry VIII
memes.description: 'Henry VIII (1491–1547) was King of England and Lord of Ireland who declared himself King of Ireland in 1542 and initiated the Tudor Reformation in Ireland.'

memes.label: Home Rule
memes.description: 'The movement for Irish self-government within the United Kingdom, the dominant political issue in Ireland from 1870 to 1918.'

memes.label: Hubert de Burgh
memes.description: 'Chief Justiciary of England and de facto ruler during the minority of Henry III, whose family became one of the most powerful Anglo-Norman dynasties in Ireland.'

memes.label: Hugh de Lacy
memes.description: 'Anglo-Norman nobleman, first Lord of Meath, and Lord Deputy of Ireland under Henry II, who sought to carve out an independent lordship in Ireland.'

memes.label: Hugh McMahon
memes.description: 'Irish chieftain of Oriel and colonel in the 1641 Rising, who played a key role in the planning of the rebellion by bringing Owen Roe O`Neil to the conspirators.'

memes.label: Hugh O'Byrne
memes.description: 'Senior chief of the OByrne clan of Wicklow, father of Feagh Mac Hugh OByrne, and a military commander of the Catholic Confederation in the 1640s.'

memes.label: Hugh ONeill
memes.description: 'Earl of Tyrone and the last great Gaelic lord of Ulster who led the Nine Years War against Elizabeth I.'

memes.label: Hugh Roe O'Donnell
memes.description: 'Gaelic Irish lord of Tyrconnell and principal commander of the Irish confederacy during the Nine Years War against Elizabethan England.'

memes.label: 'Hussey Burgh'
memes.description: 'Irish parliamentarian and patriot who was among the new generation of reformist MPs elected in 1761 alongside Henry Flood, and a key supporter of Henry Grattan''s declaration of Irish legislative independence in 1782.'

memes.label: Irish Parliament
memes.description: 'The Irish Parliament was the legislature of the Kingdom of Ireland from the 13th century until the Act of Union of 1800, evolving from an Anglo-Irish assembly representing the colonial community into a Protestant-dominated body that enacted the Penal Laws and was ultimately dissolved in favor of direct Westminster representation.'

memes.label: James Butler, Earl of Ormond
memes.description: 'Anglo-Irish statesman and royalist commander who served as Lord Lieutenant of Ireland under both Charles I and Charles II; one of the principal statesmen of the 17th century.'

memes.label: James Duff
memes.description: 'British military commander during the Irish Rebellion of 1798, known for his role in the Wexford campaign and controversial conduct at the Curragh.'

memes.label: James Hope
memes.description: 'United Irishman of Templepatrick, a faithful emissary of Robert Emmet who survived the 1798 and 1803 rebellions.'

memes.label: James I
memes.description: 'James I (1566–1625) was King of England and Ireland from 1603 to 1625; his reign saw the Flight of the Earls, the Plantation of Ulster, and the introduction of Penal Laws against Catholics.'

memes.label: James II
memes.description: "James II (1633-1701) was King of England, Scotland, and Ireland, whose efforts to restore Catholicism led to his defeat by William III at the Battle of the Boyne."

memes.label: John de Courcy
memes.description: 'Anglo-Norman knight who conquered Ulster, first Earl of Ulster, and the most celebrated adventurer of the early Norman period in Ireland.'

memes.label: 'John Fitzgibbon, Earl of Clare'
memes.description: 'Irish lawyer and politician who served as Attorney-General and Lord Chancellor of Ireland; a leading opponent of Catholic relief and architect of the Act of Union.'

memes.label: 'John Keogh'
memes.description: 'Irish Catholic merchant and political leader who led the Catholic Committee through the 1790s; a key strategist of the pre-Emancipation Catholic movement.'

memes.label: John Moore
memes.description: 'Irish rebel who was appointed President of the Provisional Government of Connacht during the French expedition of General Humbert in 1798.'

memes.label: John Perrot
memes.description: 'English soldier and Lord Deputy of Ireland, reputed illegitimate son of Henry VIII, who served as President of Munster and later Lord Deputy during the 1580s.'

memes.label: Jonathan Swift
memes.description: "Jonathan Swift (1667–1745) was an Anglo-Irish satirist, essayist, and cleric who served as Dean of St. Patrick's Cathedral, Dublin, and authored Gulliver's Travels."

memes.label: Laurence OToole
memes.description: 'Archbishop of Dublin, statesman, and saint who served as mediator between the Irish kings and the Anglo-Norman invaders during the critical period of the Norman invasion.'

memes.label: Leinster
memes.description: 'Leinster is one of the four historic provinces of Ireland, located in the east and southeast of the island, encompassing the ancient kingdoms of the Laigin people and serving as the seat of English administration from the Norman invasion onward.'

memes.label: 'Randal MacDonnell, Earl of Antrim'
memes.description: 'Catholic peer and military leader during the Confederate Wars, whose regiment was famously shut out of Derry in 1688.'

memes.label: Lord Broghill
memes.description: 'Roger Boyle, Baron Broghill, later 1st Earl of Orrery — son of the Earl of Cork, Cromwellian commander in Munster, and key figure in the Restoration settlement in Ireland.'

memes.label: Lord Camden
memes.description: 'Lord Lieutenant of Ireland from 1795 to 1798, succeeded Lord Fitzwilliam; his coercive administration preceded the Rebellion of 1798.'

memes.label: Lord Castlereagh
memes.description: 'Anglo-Irish statesman who as Chief Secretary for Ireland engineered the passage of the Act of Union in 1800, and later served as British Foreign Secretary at the Congress of Vienna.'

memes.label: Lord Charlemont
memes.description: 'James Caulfeild, 1st Earl of Charlemont, commander-in-chief of the Irish Volunteers and a leading figure in the Patriot Party of the late 18th century.'

memes.label: Lord Cornwallis
memes.description: 'Lord Lieutenant of Ireland during the Rebellion of 1798 and the Act of Union 1800, known for his surrender at Yorktown in the American Revolutionary War.'

memes.label: Lord Falkland
memes.description: 'Henry Carey, 1st Viscount Falkland, served as Lord Deputy of Ireland from 1622 to 1629 under James I and Charles I, pursuing a policy of religious toleration tempered by political duplicity.'

memes.label: Lord Fingal
memes.description: 'Earl of Fingall, prominent Catholic peer and leader in the campaign for Catholic emancipation; chairman of the Catholic Board in early 19th-century Irish politics.'

memes.label: Lord Fitzwilliam
memes.description: '2nd Earl Fitzwilliam, Lord Lieutenant of Ireland in 1795 whose brief viceroyalty ended in recall after advocating Catholic emancipation.'

memes.label: Lord Gormanstown
memes.description: 'Anglo-Irish Catholic peer, Viscount Gormanston, who played a key role in bringing the Pale lords into the Confederate Catholic cause in 1641.'

memes.label: 'Oliver St John, Viscount Grandison'
memes.description: 'Lord Deputy of Ireland from 1616 to 1622, noted for the Commission for the Discovery of Defective Titles.'

memes.label: Lord Howth
memes.description: 'Anglo-Irish peer of the St. Lawrence family, Baron of Howth, involved in the entrapment of the northern Earls and the politics of the early Stuart period.'

memes.label: 'Lord Kenmare'
memes.description: 'Catholic peer and loyalist leader, signatory of the Catholic petition and intermediary during the Union negotiations.'

memes.label: Lord Kilwarden
memes.description: 'Lord Chief Justice of Ireland, killed during Robert Emmet'\''s rising in Dublin on 23 July 1803.'

memes.label: Lord Lake
memes.description: 'General Gerard Lake, Viscount Lake (1744–1808), Commander-in-Chief in Ireland during the suppression of the Rebellion of 1798.'

memes.label: Lord Leonard Gray
memes.description: 'Lord Deputy of Ireland from 1535 to 1540, maternal uncle of Silken Thomas, who suppressed the Kildare rebellion, summoned the Reformation Parliament of 1537, and was ultimately executed for treason.'

memes.label: Lord Moore
memes.description: 'Anglo-Irish military commander who served the Crown during the 1641 Rebellion, fought alongside Sir Henry Tichburne and General Monk, and was killed in action against Owen Roe O''Neill.'

memes.label: 'Justin McCarthy, Viscount Mountcashel'
memes.description: 'Jacobite commander under James II, led the first Irish brigade in French service, died of wounds received at the Battle of Staffarda.'

memes.label: Lord Muskerry
memes.description: 'Donough MacCarty, Viscount Muskerry (later Earl of Clancarty), Confederate Catholic leader in Munster during the Wars of the Confederation and signatory to the Ormond Peace of 1646.'

memes.label: Lord North
memes.description: 'Frederick North, 2nd Earl of Guilford (1732–1792), British Prime Minister (1770–1782) during the Irish Free Trade crisis and the achievement of legislative independence.'

memes.label: Lord Portlester
memes.description: 'Anglo-Irish nobleman, Chancellor and Treasurer of Ireland, and father-in-law to Gerald FitzGerald, 8th Earl of Kildare, who played a central role in the Yorkist politics of fifteenth-century Ireland.'

memes.label: 'Lord Ranelagh'
memes.description: 'President of Connaught and Paymaster-General, active in Irish affairs during the Williamite period.'

memes.label: Lord Roden
memes.description: 'Robert Jocelyn, 2nd Earl of Roden, Irish peer and military commander whose dragoons were active in suppressing the Rebellion of 1798 and the Emmet insurrection of 1803.'

memes.label: 'Thomas Radclyffe, Earl of Sussex'
memes.description: 'Lord Deputy of Ireland under Mary I and Elizabeth I, known for his military campaigns against Shane O''Neill.'

memes.label: 'Lord Townsend'
memes.description: 'Lord Lieutenant of Ireland from 1767 to 1772, known for his controversial methods of parliamentary management.'

memes.label: Maelmurra
memes.description: 'Prince of Leinster whose defection from Brian Boru precipitated the Battle of Clontarf in 1014.'

memes.label: Magnus Barefoot
memes.description: 'King of Norway (1093–1103) who led military expeditions to the Hebrides and Ireland, allied with Murtogh O'Brien at Kinkora, and was slain in Ulster in 1103.'

memes.label: 'Malachy I'
memes.description: 'High King of Ireland (A.D. 845–860), ancestor of the OMelaghlin dynasty; a powerful ruler of the Southern Uí Néill who defended Ireland against the Norse incursions of the ninth century.'

memes.label: Malachy II
memes.description: 'Malachy II (Mael Sechnaill mac Domnaill, 948–1022) was High King of Ireland who fought alongside Brian Boru at Clontarf and twice held the supreme kingship.'

memes.label: Margaret O'Carroll
memes.description: 'A 15th-century Irish noblewoman of the O''Carroll family, celebrated for her extraordinary hospitality to the bards of Ireland and Scotland, her piety, and her public works including bridges, highways, and churches.'

memes.label: Maurice Fitzgerald
memes.description: 'Norman-Welsh adventurer, son of Nesta and Gerald of Windsor, common ancestor of the Earls of Desmond and Kildare, and progenitor of the Geraldine dynasty in Ireland.'

memes.label: Michael Dwyer
memes.description: 'The Wicklow outlaw who held out in the Wicklow Mountains after the 1798 Rebellion and later died in Australian exile.'

memes.label: Milo de Cogan
memes.description: 'Norman adventurer, co-leader of the first Norman expedition to Ireland in 1169, first governor of Dublin, and joint ruler of Desmond.'

memes.label: 'Morrogh mac Brian'
memes.description: 'Eldest son of Brian Boru, commander of the Irish forces at the Battle of Clontarf (1014), where both he and his father perished.'

memes.label: 'Mountgarrett, Viscount'
memes.description: 'Anglo-Irish Catholic nobleman and President of the Supreme Council of the Confederate Catholics of Ireland during the 1640s.'

memes.label: Murrogh O'Brien
memes.description: 'Last Gaelic lord and first Earl of Thomond, who surrendered his lordship to Henry VIII under the policy of Surrender and Regrant and was created earl in 1543, marking the transition of the O`Brien dynasty from Gaelic kingship to Anglo-Irish peerage.'

memes.label: Murtogh O'Brien
memes.description: 'High King of Ireland and King of Munster (d. 1119), grandson of Brian Boru, dominant political figure in early 12th-century Ireland'

memes.label: Napper Tandy
memes.description: 'Dublin merchant and United Irishmen leader who served as Secretary of the Dublin Society, commanded the National Guard, and died in exile after the 1798 Rebellion.'

memes.label: Navigation Acts
memes.description: 'British trade laws that restricted Irish commerce, originally devised under Cromwell and later extended, forming a central grievance in Irish economic history.'

memes.label: Neil O'Neil
memes.description: 'Irish Jacobite military commander who fell mortally wounded at the Battle of the Boyne in 1690, commanding the Irish forces at the ford of Oldbridge against the army of William III.'

memes.label: Nine Years' War
memes.description: "The Irish war of 1594–1603 led by Hugh O'Neill, Earl of Tyrone, against English rule, ending in defeat at Kinsale and the Flight of the Earls."

memes.label: Norman Invasion of Ireland
memes.description: 'The Anglo-Norman invasion of Ireland (1169–1172), initiated by Dermid McMurrogh and culminating in Henry IIs arrival.'

memes.label: Oliver Bond
memes.description: 'Prosperous Dublin merchant and leading member of the United Irishmen, whose house in Bridge Street served as the meeting-place of the Leinster executive until its seizure in March 1798.'

memes.label: Oliver Cromwell
memes.description: 'Lord Protector of England who led a devastating nine-month campaign in Ireland (1649–1650).'

memes.label: 'Orange Order'
memes.description: 'A Protestant fraternal organization founded in 1795, dedicated to the defense of Protestantism and the Union with Great Britain.'

memes.label: Owen Roe ONeill
memes.description: 'Irish Confederate general and nephew of Hugh ONeill who led the Ulster army during the Confederate Wars.'

memes.label: Patrick Sarsfield
memes.description: 'Irish Jacobite general who led the defence of Limerick during the Williamite War and negotiated the Treaty of Limerick.'

memes.label: Penal Laws
memes.description: 'A series of laws imposing civil disabilities and religious restrictions on Roman Catholics in Ireland from 1695 to 1829.'

memes.label: Plantation of Munster
memes.description: 'The Tudor-era scheme to colonize the province of Munster with English settlers, following the confiscation of lands after the Desmond Rebellions.'

memes.label: Plantation of Ulster
memes.description: 'The Plantation of Ulster was the organized colonization of the northern province of Ireland by English and Scottish settlers, begun in 1609 under King James I, which reshaped the demographic, political, and religious character of the region.'

memes.label: Pope John XXII
memes.description: 'Pope from 1316 to 1334, recipient of the celebrated Remonstrance of Donald ONeil against Anglo-Norman rule in Ireland.'

memes.label: Presbyterian
memes.description: 'The Presbyterian community in Ireland, established through Scottish settlement in Ulster and shaped by the religious and political conflicts of the 17th and 18th centuries.'

memes.label: Protestant Ascendancy
memes.description: 'The Protestant Ascendancy was the political, economic, and social domination of Ireland by the minority Protestant landowning class from the late seventeenth century through the eighteenth century.'

memes.label: Reformation
memes.description: 'The Protestant Reformation in Ireland was the process by which the established Church of Ireland was brought under royal supremacy and Protestant doctrine during the Tudor period, with profound and lasting consequences for Irish religious and political identity.'

memes.label: Repeal Movement
memes.description: 'The political campaign led by Daniel OConnell in the 1830s and 1840s to repeal the Act of Union and restore an Irish Parliament.'

memes.label: Restoration
memes.description: 'The Restoration (1660) marked the return of King Charles II to the thrones of England, Scotland, and Ireland following the collapse of the Cromwellian Commonwealth, initiating a complex period of partial Catholic recovery and contested land settlements.'

memes.label: Richard Belling
memes.description: 'Irish lawyer, writer, and secretary to the Supreme Council of the Catholic Confederation (1642–1649).'

memes.label: 'Richard Boyle, Earl of Cork'
memes.description: 'English-born adventurer who became the first Earl of Cork, amassing immense wealth through the Munster Plantation and becoming one of the most powerful figures in 17th-century Ireland.'

memes.label: Richard de Burgo
memes.description: 'Richard de Burgo, called the Red Earl of Ulster, the greatest Norman-Irish magnate of the early 14th century, Lord of Connaught and central figure in the Bruce invasion period.'

memes.label: Richard II of England
memes.description: 'King of England (1377–1399) who led two military expeditions to Ireland, the first English monarch to visit the island since the Norman invasion, and whose deposition had lasting consequences for the Anglo-Irish lordship.'

memes.label: Richard Lalor Shiel
memes.description: 'Irish politician, writer, and orator, prominent ally of Daniel OConnell in the Catholic Emancipation movement'

memes.label: 'Richard Talbot, Earl of Tyrconnell'
memes.description: 'Irish Catholic soldier and Jacobite Lord Deputy of Ireland under James II; a central figure in the Williamite War in Ireland.'

memes.label: 'Giovanni Battista Rinuccini'
memes.description: 'Italian Catholic archbishop who served as Papal Nuncio to the Irish Confederate Catholics during the Wars of the Three Kingdoms; a key political and religious actor in the Confederate period.'

memes.label: Robert Emmet
memes.description: "Robert Emmet (1778-1803) was an Irish nationalist who led an unsuccessful rebellion against British rule in 1803, celebrated for his speech from the dock before his execution."

memes.label: Robert Fitzstephen
memes.description: 'Cambro-Norman knight and one of the first Norman invaders of Ireland, who led the vanguard of Strongbow''s expedition in 1169.'

memes.label: Rory OConor
memes.description: "Rory OConor (c.1116-1198), also known as Roderick OConor, was the last High King of Ireland before the Anglo-Norman invasion."

memes.label: Rory O'Donnell
memes.description: 'Gaelic Irish nobleman and Earl of Tyrconnell who succeeded Hugh Roe ODonnell and fled Ireland in the Flight of the Earls.'

memes.label: 'Rory O''Moore'
memes.description: 'Irish chieftain of the O''Moore clan and a principal leader of the Irish Rebellion of 1641 in Leinster, renowned for his rapid and daring military campaigns.'

memes.label: Saint Adamnan
memes.description: 'Abbot of Iona, hagiographer of Saint Columba, and lawmaker whose influence shaped both Ireland and Scotland in the seventh century.'

memes.label: Saint Aidan
memes.description: 'Saint Aidan (d. 651), the Apostle of Northumbria, was an Irish missionary monk from Iona who founded the monastery of Lindisfarne and led the conversion of the Anglo-Saxon kingdom of Northumbria to Christianity.'

memes.label: Saint Brendan the Navigator
memes.description: 'Early Irish monastic saint and navigator, founder of the monastery at Clonfert, remembered for his legendary Atlantic voyages'

memes.label: Saint Columba
memes.description: 'Saint Columba (521–597), also known as Columkille, was an Irish abbot and missionary who founded the monastery of Iona and led the conversion of Scotland to Christianity.'

memes.label: Saint Columbanus
memes.description: 'Irish missionary monk and scholar who founded monasteries across Burgundy and Lombardy, one of the most influential figures of the Irish monastic diaspora in early medieval Europe.'

memes.label: Saint Kieran of Clonmacnoise
memes.description: 'Early Irish saint and abbot, founder of the monastery of Clonmacnoise in the sixth century, one of the twelve apostles of Ireland'

memes.label: Saint Malachy
memes.description: 'Twelfth-century Irish saint, Archbishop of Armagh, and Primate of Ireland who led the reform of the Irish Church and was canonized by Pope Clement III in 1190.'

memes.label: Saint Moling
memes.description: 'Seventh-century Irish saint of the royal house of Leinster, Bishop of Ferns, who secured the permanent abolition of the Borooa tribute through his spiritual authority and prophetic vision.'

memes.label: Saint Patrick
memes.description: 'The Apostle of Ireland who converted the Irish to Christianity in the 5th century.'

memes.label: Saint Ruadan
memes.description: 'Sixth-century Irish saint and abbot who pronounced the curse upon the royal seat of Tara in 554, leading to its abandonment by the High Kings of Ireland.'

memes.label: Saint Ruth
memes.description: 'French general sent by Louis XIV to command the Jacobite army in Ireland; killed at the Battle of Aughrim, the decisive engagement of the Williamite War.'

memes.label: Samuel Neilson
memes.description: 'Proprietor of the Northern Star newspaper and a founding member of the United Irishmen in Belfast.'

memes.label: 'Schomberg, Duke of'
memes.description: 'German-born Huguenot general who commanded the Williamite army in Ireland during the early phase of the Williamite War; killed at the Battle of the Boyne.'

memes.label: 'Siege of Limerick'
memes.description: 'The Siege of Limerick (1690–1691) was the climactic military engagement of the Williamite War in Ireland, ending with the Treaty of Limerick which promised religious liberty to Irish Catholics—a promise subsequently broken.'

memes.label: Simon Butler
memes.description: 'Irish barrister and founding chairman of the Dublin Society of United Irishmen, imprisoned in 1792 for his advocacy of parliamentary reform.'

memes.label: 'Sir Anthony St. Leger'
memes.description: 'English administrator who served multiple terms as Lord Deputy of Ireland under Henry VIII, Edward VI, and Mary I, overseeing the constitutional change that made Henry King of Ireland and the first attempts to introduce the Protestant Reformation.'

memes.label: 'Sir Arthur Chichester'
memes.description: 'English soldier and administrator who served as Lord Deputy of Ireland from 1604 to 1615, overseeing the Flight of the Earls, the Plantation of Ulster, and the abolition of native Irish legal customs.'

memes.label: Sir Charles Coote
memes.description: 'English-born soldier and administrator who served as President of Connaught during the Irish Rebellion of 1641, remembered for the ruthlessness of his campaign against the Catholic insurgents.'

memes.label: Sir Conyers Clifford
memes.description: 'English soldier and Governor of Connaught during the Nine Years'' War, who was killed at the Battle of the Curlew Mountains in 1599 while attempting to relieve O''Conor Sligo.'

memes.label: 'Sir James Fitzmaurice'
memes.description: 'Leader of the first Desmond Rebellion, known for his indomitable resistance to Elizabethan authority and his Papal-backed invasion of Munster.'

memes.label: Sir John Davis
memes.description: 'Attorney-General for Ireland under James I, author of influential tracts on Irish affairs, and a principal superintendent of legal reforms in early 17th-century Ireland.'

memes.label: Sir John Norris
memes.description: 'English military commander in Elizabethan Ireland, President of Munster, served during the Nine Years War.'

memes.label: Sir John Stanley
memes.description: 'English knight who served multiple terms as Lord Deputy and Lord Lieutenant of Ireland during the reigns of Richard II and Henry IV in the late 14th and early 15th centuries.'

memes.label: Sir Lawrence Parsons
memes.description: 'Anglo-Irish politician and Birr Castle landowner; Sir Lawrence Parsons of Parsonstown opposed the Act of Union and supported Catholic relief, serving in the Irish Parliament in its final decades.'

memes.label: Sir Phelim O'Neill
memes.description: 'Leader of the 1641 Rebellion in Ulster and first commander of the Confederate Catholic forces in the north of Ireland.'

memes.label: Sir Richard Nagle
memes.description: 'Irish Jacobite lawyer and politician, Attorney-General for Ireland under James II, author of the famous "Coventry Letter" defending Irish Catholic claims.'

memes.label: Sir Toby Butler
memes.description: 'Irish Jacobite lawyer who served as Solicitor-General for Ireland under James II and helped negotiate the Treaty of Limerick.'

memes.label: Sir Walter Fitzgerald
memes.description: 'A 16th-century Anglo-Irish nobleman, son-in-law to Feagh Mac Hugh O''Byrne, who took refuge in Glenmalure after supporting the fugitive Viscount Baltinglass against the Dublin government.'

memes.label: 'Sir William Drury'
memes.description: 'English soldier and administrator who served as Lord Justice and Lord Deputy of Ireland under Queen Elizabeth I, dying in the field during the Desmond Rebellion of 1579.'

memes.label: Sitrick
memes.description: 'Norse-Gaelic King of Dublin, son-in-law of Brian Boru, fought at the Battle of Clontarf (1014).'

memes.label: Strongbow
memes.description: 'Richard de Clare, 2nd Earl of Pembroke (1130–1176), known as Strongbow, was the Norman lord whose invasion of Ireland in 1170 initiated the Anglo-Norman conquest.'

memes.label: 'Surrender and Regrant'
memes.description: 'Surrender and Regrant was a Tudor policy in Ireland by which Gaelic and Anglo-Irish lords surrendered their lands to the Crown and received them back under English feudal law, transforming traditional Irish lordship into English legal tenure.'

memes.label: Tara
memes.description: 'The ancient seat of the High Kings of Ireland, located in County Meath.'

memes.label: Teague O'Regan
memes.description: 'Irish Jacobite commander who conducted a prolonged defence of Charlemont during the Williamite War and was later knighted for his valour.'

memes.label: Thaddeus O'Brien
memes.description: 'Prince of Thomond and claimant to the sovereignty of Munster, active in the twelfth and fifteenth centuries against Norman and Crown authority.'

memes.label: Theobald Wolfe Tone
memes.description: 'Irish revolutionary and founding member of the United Irishmen who led the rebellion of 1798 with French support.'

memes.label: Thomas Addis Emmet
memes.description: 'Irish patriot and United Irishmen leader, brother of Robert Emmet, who served as plenipotentiary in Paris during the Napoleonic era.'

memes.label: Thomas de Clare
memes.description: 'Anglo-Norman lord, son of the Earl of Gloucester, granted the lordship of Thomond by Edward I; killed in battle against Turlogh O`Brien in 1287.'

memes.label: Silken Thomas
memes.description: 'Thomas Fitzgerald, 10th Earl of Kildare, known as Silken Thomas, who led an abortive rebellion against Henry VIII in 1534 and was executed at Tyburn in 1537.'

memes.label: 'Thomas Russell'
memes.description: 'Irish revolutionary and United Irishman executed in 1803; a close associate of Theobald Wolfe Tone and Robert Emmet, and a founding figure of the United Irish movement.'
aliases: Thomas Russell of the United Irishmen

memes.label: 'Thomas Wentworth, Earl of Strafford'
memes.description: 'English statesman and Lord Deputy of Ireland whose policy of "Thorough" aimed at absolute royal authority; impeached and executed by the English Parliament in 1641.'

memes.label: 'Thorlogh More O''Conor'
memes.description: 'High King of Ireland (r. 1136–1156), the first monarch to maintain a regular fleet at sea and the father of Roderick O''Conor, the last High King.'

memes.label: Thorlogh O'Brien
memes.description: 'King of Munster and rival to Thorlogh More O`Conor, defeated at the Battle of Moanmore in 1151, ending O`Brien dominance of the Irish high-kingship.'

memes.label: Tiernan O'Ruarc
memes.description: 'Lord of Breffni whose conflict with Dermid McMurrogh contributed to the circumstances leading to the Norman invasion of Ireland.'

memes.label: Tuathal the Legitimate
memes.description: 'Ancient High King of Ireland, surnamed "the Legitimate," who imposed the celebrated Boru tribute on the province of Leinster.'

memes.label: Turlogh O'Brien
memes.description: 'Irish nobleman of the O`Brien dynasty, son of the Lord of Thomond, who served as a military commander under the Earl of Clanrickarde during the Nine Years` War and the early 17th-century conflicts.'

memes.label: Ulster
memes.description: 'Ulster is the northernmost of the four historic provinces of Ireland, renowned as the center of ancient Irish epic literature, the last Gaelic stronghold against English conquest, and the focal point of the Plantation of Ulster which reshaped its demographic and political character.'

memes.label: United Irishmen
memes.description: 'The revolutionary republican organization founded in 1791 that led the Irish Rebellion of 1798.'

memes.label: Virgilius of Saltzburgh
memes.description: 'Irish missionary, Bishop of Saltzburgh, and astronomer of the 8th century who defended the sphericity of the earth against St. Boniface and was later canonized.'

memes.label: Walter de Burgh
memes.description: 'Anglo-Norman lord of Connaught in the 13th century, progenitor of the powerful de Burgh family in Ireland, who died in 1271 in the Castle of Galway.'

memes.label: Walter Devereux, Earl of Essex
memes.description: 'English nobleman and adventurer who obtained a grant of territory in Ulster in 1573 and led an ill-fated plantation attempt; father of Robert Devereux, 2nd Earl of Essex.'

memes.label: William de Burgh
memes.description: 'Powerful Anglo-Norman baron who established the de Burgh family in Connaught, progenitor of the Burkes of Connaught and the McWilliam line.'

memes.label: William de Windsor
memes.description: 'English knight who served as Lord Lieutenant of Ireland during the reign of Edward III, known for his defensive governance amid the Gaelic resurgence of the late fourteenth century.'

memes.label: William Drennan
memes.description: 'Physician and poet-politician of the United Irishmen who wrote the "Universal Emancipation" address, for which his friend was convicted, and which John Philpot Curran immortalized in his famous defence speech.'

memes.label: William III
memes.description: 'King of England, Scotland, and Ireland from 1689 to 1702, whose reign in Ireland was defined by the Williamite War, the Battle of the Boyne, the Treaty of Limerick, and the establishment of the Protestant Ascendancy.'

memes.label: William James McNevin
memes.description: 'Connaught Catholic physician educated in Austria, United Irishman leader, arrested in March 1798, later emigrated to New York where he became a prominent advocate for Catholic Emancipation.'

memes.label: William Molyneux
memes.description: 'Anglo-Irish philosopher and political writer, author of "The Case of Ireland Stated" (1698), a foundational argument for Irish legislative independence.'

memes.label: 'William Petty'
memes.description: 'English economist, scientist, and surveyor who directed the Down Survey of Ireland (1654–1659), the first systematic cadastral survey of confiscated Irish lands, and founded the house of Lansdowne.'

memes.label: 'William Pitt the Younger'
memes.description: 'British Prime Minister (1783–1801, 1804–1806) whose implacable enmity toward the Irish Parliament after the Regency Crisis of 1789 became a determining cause of the Act of Union, and who wielded immense influence over Irish affairs until his death in 1806.'

memes.label: William Sampson
memes.description: 'An Irish lawyer and United Irishman, born in 1764, arrested with Lord Edward Fitzgerald in March 1798, imprisoned and later expatriated to New York after the Peace of Amiens.'
